{{Infobox ancient site | name =Odara Kofun | native_name =小田良古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Odara Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|38|57|N|130|29|37|E|display=inline, title|ISO =JP-43}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Uki, Kumamoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|38|57|N|130|29|37|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Odara Kofun'''|小田良古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the [[Misumi, Kumamoto|Misumi]] neighborhood of the city of [[Uki, Kumamoto|Uki]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139694|title= 小田良古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Odara Kofun is located at the tip of a low hill close to the coast on the northern coast of the [[Uto Peninsula]], which juts out west from the center of the prefecture. This tumulu is thought to have been an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tumulus with a diameter of about 20 meters, but most of the mound has been lost, and materials for the stone [[burial chamber]] have been piled up. [[Archaeological excavation]]s conducted in 1978 confirmed that the area above the tomb is now farmland 7.2 meters above sea level, and the earth had been removed to the point where the upper edge of the stone barrier around the stone chamber was visible on the field. Four sides of the barrier remain, measuring 1.9 meters east–west and 1.85 meters north–south, with the height to the gravel bed inside being 0.65 meters. The barrier is made of shaped sandstone and is 8 centimeters thick. The inside of the stone barrier is divided by two rows of partition stones running east to west, and two sections of corpse beds are set on the north–south and west sides, with round stones the size of a fist laid on the bottom. The central passage between the corpse beds is slightly lower than the corpse beds. Around this stone barrier, some of the piled stones from the walls of the stone chamber remain. The burial chamber was constructed by digging a pit measuring 3.5 meters east to west and 3.2 meters north to south. The entrance to the burial chamber is thought to face west, and there is a V-shaped recess on the west side of the stone barrier. There is also a shallow V-shaped recess in the center of the two rows of partition stones.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The four sides on the stone barrier are decorated with carvings and colors, with four circles on the north side, three circles, two ridges, and two shields on the east side of the back wall, three circles on the south side, and two circles on the west side, all of which are contained within two horizontal lines. The circles are 17-18 centimeters in diameter, with a central hole carved out, two string-like expressions on the top and bottom, and the shield is decorated with patterns, making it important as a stone barrier-type [[decorated kofun]]. There is also a red paint that appears to have been applied to the entire tomb, but its condition is poor. Excavated [[grave goods]] include knives, small beads, and bells, as well as fragments of human bones. The construction and decorative patterns suggest that the tumulus was built around the late 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/> In order to prioritize preservation, not everything was excavated, and damaged stone was treated for preservation before being backfilled.Currently, only the outside of the stone chamber can be viewed under the roof. A life-size replica of the interior of the stone chamber, which cannot be seen on-site, is on display at the Kumamoto Prefectural Decorated Burial Mounds Museum.
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

{{nihongo|'''Odara Kofun'''|小田良古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the [[Misumi, Kumamoto|Misumi]] neighborhood of the city of [[Uki, Kumamoto|Uki]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139694|title= 小田良古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Odara Kofun is located at the tip of a low hill close to the coast on the northern coast of the [[Uto Peninsula]], which juts out west from the center of the prefecture. This tumulu is thought to have been an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tumulus with a diameter of about 20 meters, but most of the mound has been lost, and materials for the stone [[burial chamber]] have been piled up. [[Archaeological excavation]]s conducted in 1978 confirmed that the area above the tomb is now farmland 7.2 meters above sea level, and the earth had been removed to the point where the upper edge of the stone barrier around the stone chamber was visible on the field. Four sides of the barrier remain, measuring 1.9 meters east–west and 1.85 meters north–south, with the height to the gravel bed inside being 0.65 meters. The barrier is made of shaped sandstone and is 8 centimeters thick. The inside of the stone barrier is divided by two rows of partition stones running east to west, and two sections of corpse beds are set on the north–south and west sides, with round stones the size of a fist laid on the bottom. The central passage between the corpse beds is slightly lower than the corpse beds. Around this stone barrier, some of the piled stones from the walls of the stone chamber remain. The burial chamber was constructed by digging a pit measuring 3.5 meters east to west and 3.2 meters north to south. The entrance to the burial chamber is thought to face west, and there is a V-shaped recess on the west side of the stone barrier. There is also a shallow V-shaped recess in the center of the two rows of partition stones.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The four sides on the stone barrier are decorated with carvings and colors, with four circles on the north side, three circles, two ridges, and two shields on the east side of the back wall, three circles on the south side, and two circles on the west side, all of which are contained within two horizontal lines. The circles are 17-18 centimeters in diameter, with a central hole carved out, two string-like expressions on the top and bottom, and the shield is decorated with patterns, making it important as a stone barrier-type [[decorated kofun]]. There is also a red paint that appears to have been applied to the entire tomb, but its condition is poor. Excavated [[grave goods]] include knives, small beads, and bells, as well as fragments of human bones. The construction and decorative patterns suggest that the tumulus was built around the late 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/> In order to prioritize preservation, not everything was excavated, and damaged stone was treated for preservation before being backfilled.Currently, only the outside of the stone chamber can be viewed under the roof. A life-size replica of the interior of the stone chamber, which cannot be seen on-site, is on display at the Kumamoto Prefectural Decorated Burial Mounds Museum.

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Odara Kofun | native_name =小田良古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Odara Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|38|57|N|130|29|37|E|display=inline, title|ISO =JP-43}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Uki, Kumamoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|38|57|N|130|29|37|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Odara Kofun'''|小田良古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the [[Misumi, Kumamoto|Misumi]] neighborhood of the city of [[Uki, Kumamoto|Uki]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139694|title= 小田良古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Odara Kofun is located at the tip of a low hill close to the coast on the northern coast of the [[Uto Peninsula]], which juts out west from the center of the prefecture. This tumulu is thought to have been an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tumulus with a diameter of about 20 meters, but most of the mound has been lost, and materials for the stone [[burial chamber]] have been piled up. [[Archaeological excavation]]s conducted in 1978 confirmed that the area above the tomb is now farmland 7.2 meters above sea level, and the earth had been removed to the point where the upper edge of the stone barrier around the stone chamber was visible on the field. Four sides of the barrier remain, measuring 1.9 meters east–west and 1.85 meters north–south, with the height to the gravel bed inside being 0.65 meters. The barrier is made of shaped sandstone and is 8 centimeters thick. The inside of the stone barrier is divided by two rows of partition stones running east to west, and two sections of corpse beds are set on the north–south and west sides, with round stones the size of a fist laid on the bottom. The central passage between the corpse beds is slightly lower than the corpse beds. Around this stone barrier, some of the piled stones from the walls of the stone chamber remain. The burial chamber was constructed by digging a pit measuring 3.5 meters east to west and 3.2 meters north to south. The entrance to the burial chamber is thought to face west, and there is a V-shaped recess on the west side of the stone barrier. There is also a shallow V-shaped recess in the center of the two rows of partition stones.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The four sides on the stone barrier are decorated with carvings and colors, with four circles on the north side, three circles, two ridges, and two shields on the east side of the back wall, three circles on the south side, and two circles on the west side, all of which are contained within two horizontal lines. The circles are 17-18 centimeters in diameter, with a central hole carved out, two string-like expressions on the top and bottom, and the shield is decorated with patterns, making it important as a stone barrier-type [[decorated kofun]]. There is also a red paint that appears to have been applied to the entire tomb, but its condition is poor. Excavated [[grave goods]] include knives, small beads, and bells, as well as fragments of human bones. The construction and decorative patterns suggest that the tumulus was built around the late 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/> In order to prioritize preservation, not everything was excavated, and damaged stone was treated for preservation before being backfilled.Currently, only the outside of the stone chamber can be viewed under the roof. A life-size replica of the interior of the stone chamber, which cannot be seen on-site, is on display at the Kumamoto Prefectural Decorated Burial Mounds Museum.

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Odara Kofun | native_name =小田良古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Odara Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|38|57|N|130|29|37|E|display=inline, title|ISO =JP-43}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Uki, Kumamoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|38|57|N|130|29|37|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Odara Kofun'''|小田良古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the [[Misumi, Kumamoto|Misumi]] neighborhood of the city of [[Uki, Kumamoto|Uki]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139694|title= 小田良古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Odara Kofun is located at the tip of a low hill close to the coast on the northern coast of the [[Uto Peninsula]], which juts out west from the center of the prefecture. This tumulu is thought to have been an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tumulus with a diameter of about 20 meters, but most of the mound has been lost, and materials for the stone [[burial chamber]] have been piled up. [[Archaeological excavation]]s conducted in 1978 confirmed that the area above the tomb is now farmland 7.2 meters above sea level, and the earth had been removed to the point where the upper edge of the stone barrier around the stone chamber was visible on the field. Four sides of the barrier remain, measuring 1.9 meters east–west and 1.85 meters north–south, with the height to the gravel bed inside being 0.65 meters. The barrier is made of shaped sandstone and is 8 centimeters thick. The inside of the stone barrier is divided by two rows of partition stones running east to west, and two sections of corpse beds are set on the north–south and west sides, with round stones the size of a fist laid on the bottom. The central passage between the corpse beds is slightly lower than the corpse beds. Around this stone barrier, some of the piled stones from the walls of the stone chamber remain. The burial chamber was constructed by digging a pit measuring 3.5 meters east to west and 3.2 meters north to south. The entrance to the burial chamber is thought to face west, and there is a V-shaped recess on the west side of the stone barrier. There is also a shallow V-shaped recess in the center of the two rows of partition stones.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The four sides on the stone barrier are decorated with carvings and colors, with four circles on the north side, three circles, two ridges, and two shields on the east side of the back wall, three circles on the south side, and two circles on the west side, all of which are contained within two horizontal lines. The circles are 17-18 centimeters in diameter, with a central hole carved out, two string-like expressions on the top and bottom, and the shield is decorated with patterns, making it important as a stone barrier-type [[decorated kofun]]. There is also a red paint that appears to have been applied to the entire tomb, but its condition is poor. Excavated [[grave goods]] include knives, small beads, and bells, as well as fragments of human bones. The construction and decorative patterns suggest that the tumulus was built around the late 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/> In order to prioritize preservation, not everything was excavated, and damaged stone was treated for preservation before being backfilled.Currently, only the outside of the stone chamber can be viewed under the roof. A life-size replica of the interior of the stone chamber, which cannot be seen on-site, is on display at the Kumamoto Prefectural Decorated Burial Mounds Museum.

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Odara Kofun | native_name =小田良古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Odara Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|38|57|N|130|29|37|E|display=inline, title|ISO =JP-43}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Uki, Kumamoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|38|57|N|130|29|37|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Odara Kofun'''|小田良古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the [[Misumi, Kumamoto|Misumi]] neighborhood of the city of [[Uki, Kumamoto|Uki]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139694|title= 小田良古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Odara Kofun is located at the tip of a low hill close to the coast on the northern coast of the [[Uto Peninsula]], which juts out west from the center of the prefecture. This tumulu is thought to have been an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tumulus with a diameter of about 20 meters, but most of the mound has been lost, and materials for the stone [[burial chamber]] have been piled up. [[Archaeological excavation]]s conducted in 1978 confirmed that the area above the tomb is now farmland 7.2 meters above sea level, and the earth had been removed to the point where the upper edge of the stone barrier around the stone chamber was visible on the field. Four sides of the barrier remain, measuring 1.9 meters east–west and 1.85 meters north–south, with the height to the gravel bed inside being 0.65 meters. The barrier is made of shaped sandstone and is 8 centimeters thick. The inside of the stone barrier is divided by two rows of partition stones running east to west, and two sections of corpse beds are set on the north–south and west sides, with round stones the size of a fist laid on the bottom. The central passage between the corpse beds is slightly lower than the corpse beds. Around this stone barrier, some of the piled stones from the walls of the stone chamber remain. The burial chamber was constructed by digging a pit measuring 3.5 meters east to west and 3.2 meters north to south. The entrance to the burial chamber is thought to face west, and there is a V-shaped recess on the west side of the stone barrier. There is also a shallow V-shaped recess in the center of the two rows of partition stones.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The four sides on the stone barrier are decorated with carvings and colors, with four circles on the north side, three circles, two ridges, and two shields on the east side of the back wall, three circles on the south side, and two circles on the west side, all of which are contained within two horizontal lines. The circles are 17-18 centimeters in diameter, with a central hole carved out, two string-like expressions on the top and bottom, and the shield is decorated with patterns, making it important as a stone barrier-type [[decorated kofun]]. There is also a red paint that appears to have been applied to the entire tomb, but its condition is poor. Excavated [[grave goods]] include knives, small beads, and bells, as well as fragments of human bones. The construction and decorative patterns suggest that the tumulus was built around the late 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/> In order to prioritize preservation, not everything was excavated, and damaged stone was treated for preservation before being backfilled.Currently, only the outside of the stone chamber can be viewed under the roof. A life-size replica of the interior of the stone chamber, which cannot be seen on-site, is on display at the Kumamoto Prefectural Decorated Burial Mounds Museum.

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Odara Kofun | native_name =小田良古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Odara Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|38|57|N|130|29|37|E|display=inline, title|ISO =JP-43}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Uki, Kumamoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|38|57|N|130|29|37|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Odara Kofun'''|小田良古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the [[Misumi, Kumamoto|Misumi]] neighborhood of the city of [[Uki, Kumamoto|Uki]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139694|title= 小田良古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Odara Kofun is located at the tip of a low hill close to the coast on the northern coast of the [[Uto Peninsula]], which juts out west from the center of the prefecture. This tumulu is thought to have been an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tumulus with a diameter of about 20 meters, but most of the mound has been lost, and materials for the stone [[burial chamber]] have been piled up. [[Archaeological excavation]]s conducted in 1978 confirmed that the area above the tomb is now farmland 7.2 meters above sea level, and the earth had been removed to the point where the upper edge of the stone barrier around the stone chamber was visible on the field. Four sides of the barrier remain, measuring 1.9 meters east–west and 1.85 meters north–south, with the height to the gravel bed inside being 0.65 meters. The barrier is made of shaped sandstone and is 8 centimeters thick. The inside of the stone barrier is divided by two rows of partition stones running east to west, and two sections of corpse beds are set on the north–south and west sides, with round stones the size of a fist laid on the bottom. The central passage between the corpse beds is slightly lower than the corpse beds. Around this stone barrier, some of the piled stones from the walls of the stone chamber remain. The burial chamber was constructed by digging a pit measuring 3.5 meters east to west and 3.2 meters north to south. The entrance to the burial chamber is thought to face west, and there is a V-shaped recess on the west side of the stone barrier. There is also a shallow V-shaped recess in the center of the two rows of partition stones.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The four sides on the stone barrier are decorated with carvings and colors, with four circles on the north side, three circles, two ridges, and two shields on the east side of the back wall, three circles on the south side, and two circles on the west side, all of which are contained within two horizontal lines. The circles are 17-18 centimeters in diameter, with a central hole carved out, two string-like expressions on the top and bottom, and the shield is decorated with patterns, making it important as a stone barrier-type [[decorated kofun]]. There is also a red paint that appears to have been applied to the entire tomb, but its condition is poor. Excavated [[grave goods]] include knives, small beads, and bells, as well as fragments of human bones. The construction and decorative patterns suggest that the tumulus was built around the late 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/> In order to prioritize preservation, not everything was excavated, and damaged stone was treated for preservation before being backfilled.Currently, only the outside of the stone chamber can be viewed under the roof. A life-size replica of the interior of the stone chamber, which cannot be seen on-site, is on display at the Kumamoto Prefectural Decorated Kofuns Museum.

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Odara Kofun | native_name =小田良古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Odara Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|38|57|N|130|29|37|E|display=inline, title|ISO =JP-43}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Uki, Kumamoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|38|57|N|130|29|37|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Odara Kofun'''|小田良古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the [[Misumi, Kumamoto|Misumi]] neighborhood of the city of [[Uki, Kumamoto|Uki]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139694|title= 小田良古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Odara Kofun is located at the tip of a low hill close to the coast on the northern coast of the [[Uto Peninsula]], which juts out west from the center of the prefecture. This tumulu is thought to have been an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tumulus with a diameter of about 20 meters, but most of the mound has been lost, and materials for the stone [[burial chamber]] have been piled up. [[Archaeological excavation]]s conducted in 1978 confirmed that the area above the tomb is now farmland 7.2 meters above sea level, and the earth had been removed to the point where the upper edge of the stone barrier around the stone chamber was visible on the field. Four sides of the barrier remain, measuring 1.9 meters east–west and 1.85 meters north–south, with the height to the gravel bed inside being 0.65 meters. The barrier is made of shaped sandstone and is 8 centimeters thick. The inside of the stone barrier is divided by two rows of partition stones running east to west, and two sections of corpse beds are set on the north–south and west sides, with round stones the size of a fist laid on the bottom. The central passage between the corpse beds is slightly lower than the corpse beds. Around this stone barrier, some of the piled stones from the walls of the stone chamber remain. The burial chamber was constructed by digging a pit measuring 3.5 meters east to west and 3.2 meters north to south. The entrance to the burial chamber is thought to face west, and there is a V-shaped recess on the west side of the stone barrier. There is also a shallow V-shaped recess in the center of the two rows of partition stones.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The four sides on the stone barrier are decorated with carvings and colors, with four circles on the north side, three circles, two ridges, and two shields on the east side of the back wall, three circles on the south side, and two circles on the west side, all of which are contained within two horizontal lines. The circles are 17-18 centimeters in diameter, with a central hole carved out, two string-like expressions on the top and bottom, and the shield is decorated with patterns, making it important as a stone barrier-type [[decorated kofun]]. There is also a red paint that appears to have been applied to the entire tomb, but its condition is poor. Excavated [[grave goods]] include knives, small beads, and bells, as well as fragments of human bones. The construction and decorative patterns suggest that the tumulus was built around the late 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/> In order to prioritize preservation, not everything was excavated, and damaged stone was treated for preservation before being backfilled.Currently, only the outside of the stone chamber can be viewed under the roof. A life-size replica of the interior of the stone chamber, which cannot be seen on-site, is on display at the Kumamoto Prefectural Decorated Kofuns Museum.

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Odara Kofun | native_name =小田良古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Odara Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|38|57|N|130|29|37|E|display=inline, title|ISO =JP-43}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Uki, Kumamoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|38|57|N|130|29|37|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Odara Kofun'''|小田良古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the [[Misumi, Kumamoto|Misumi]] neighborhood of the city of [[Uki, Kumamoto|Uki]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139694|title= 小田良古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Odara Kofun is located at the tip of a low hill close to the coast on the northern coast of the [[Uto Peninsula]], which juts out west from the center of the prefecture. This tumulu is thought to have been an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tumulus with a diameter of about 20 meters, but most of the mound has been lost, and materials for the stone [[burial chamber]] have been piled up. [[Archaeological excavation]]s conducted in 1978 confirmed that the area above the tomb is now farmland 7.2 meters above sea level, and the earth had been removed to the point where the upper edge of the stone barrier around the stone chamber was visible on the field. Four sides of the barrier remain, measuring 1.9 meters east–west and 1.85 meters north–south, with the height to the gravel bed inside being 0.65 meters. The barrier is made of shaped sandstone and is 8 centimeters thick. The inside of the stone barrier is divided by two rows of partition stones running east to west, and two sections of corpse beds are set on the north–south and west sides, with round stones the size of a fist laid on the bottom. The central passage between the corpse beds is slightly lower than the corpse beds. Around this stone barrier, some of the piled stones from the walls of the stone chamber remain. The burial chamber was constructed by digging a pit measuring 3.5 meters east to west and 3.2 meters north to south. The entrance to the burial chamber is thought to face west, and there is a V-shaped recess on the west side of the stone barrier. There is also a shallow V-shaped recess in the center of the two rows of partition stones.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The four sides on the stone barrier are decorated with carvings and colors, with four circles on the north side, three circles, two ridges, and two shields on the east side of the back wall, three circles on the south side, and two circles on the west side, all of which are contained within two horizontal lines. The circles are 17-18 centimeters in diameter, with a central hole carved out, two string-like expressions on the top and bottom, and the shield is decorated with patterns, making it important as a stone barrier-type [[decorated kofun]]. There is also a red paint that appears to have been applied to the entire tomb, but its condition is poor. Excavated [[grave goods]] include knives, small beads, and bells, as well as fragments of human bones. The construction and decorative patterns suggest that the tumulus was built around the late 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/> In order to prioritize preservation, not everything was excavated, and damaged stone was treated for preservation before being backfilled.Currently, only the outside of the stone chamber can be viewed under the roof. A life-size replica of the interior of the stone chamber, which cannot be seen on-site, is on display at the Kumamoto Prefectural Decorated Kofuns Museum.

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | name =Odara Kofun | native_name =小田良古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Odara Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|38|57|N|130|29|37|E|display=inline, title|ISO =JP-43}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Uki, Kumamoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|38|57|N|130|29|37|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Odara Kofun'''|小田良古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the [[Misumi, Kumamoto|Misumi]] neighborhood of the city of [[Uki, Kumamoto|Uki]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139694|title= 小田良古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Odara Kofun is located at the tip of a low hill close to the coast on the northern coast of the [[Uto Peninsula]], which juts out west from the center of the prefecture. This tumulu is thought to have been an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tumulus with a diameter of about 20 meters, but most of the mound has been lost, and materials for the stone [[burial chamber]] have been piled up. [[Archaeological excavation]]s conducted in 1978 confirmed that the area above the tomb is now farmland 7.2 meters above sea level, and the earth had been removed to the point where the upper edge of the stone barrier around the stone chamber was visible on the field. Four sides of the barrier remain, measuring 1.9 meters east–west and 1.85 meters north–south, with the height to the gravel bed inside being 0.65 meters. The barrier is made of shaped sandstone and is 8 centimeters thick. The inside of the stone barrier is divided by two rows of partition stones running east to west, and two sections of corpse beds are set on the north–south and west sides, with round stones the size of a fist laid on the bottom. The central passage between the corpse beds is slightly lower than the corpse beds. Around this stone barrier, some of the piled stones from the walls of the stone chamber remain. The burial chamber was constructed by digging a pit measuring 3.5 meters east to west and 3.2 meters north to south. The entrance to the burial chamber is thought to face west, and there is a V-shaped recess on the west side of the stone barrier. There is also a shallow V-shaped recess in the center of the two rows of partition stones.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The four sides on the stone barrier are decorated with carvings and colors, with four circles on the north side, three circles, two ridges, and two shields on the east side of the back wall, three circles on the south side, and two circles on the west side, all of which are contained within two horizontal lines. The circles are 17-18 centimeters in diameter, with a central hole carved out, two string-like expressions on the top and bottom, and the shield is decorated with patterns, making it important as a stone barrier-type [[decorated kofun]]. There is also a red paint that appears to have been applied to the entire tomb, but its condition is poor. Excavated [[grave goods]] include knives, small beads, and bells, as well as fragments of human bones. The construction and decorative patterns suggest that the tumulus was built around the late 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/> In order to prioritize preservation, not everything was excavated, and damaged stone was treated for preservation before being backfilled.Currently, only the outside of the stone chamber can be viewed under the roof. A life-size replica of the interior of the stone chamber, which cannot be seen on-site, is on display at the Kumamoto Prefectural Decorated Kofuns Museum.

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | name =Odara Kofun | native_name =小田良古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Odara Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|38|57|N|130|29|37|E|display=inline, title|ISO =JP-43}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Uki, Kumamoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|38|57|N|130|29|37|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Odara Kofun'''|小田良古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the [[Misumi, Kumamoto|Misumi]] neighborhood of the city of [[Uki, Kumamoto|Uki]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139694|title= 小田良古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Odara Kofun is located at the tip of a low hill close to the coast on the northern coast of the [[Uto Peninsula]], which juts out west from the center of the prefecture. This tumulu is thought to have been an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tumulus with a diameter of about 20 meters, but most of the mound has been lost, and materials for the stone [[burial chamber]] have been piled up. [[Archaeological excavation]]s conducted in 1978 confirmed that the area above the tomb is now farmland 7.2 meters above sea level, and the earth had been removed to the point where the upper edge of the stone barrier around the stone chamber was visible on the field. Four sides of the barrier remain, measuring 1.9 meters east–west and 1.85 meters north–south, with the height to the gravel bed inside being 0.65 meters. The barrier is made of shaped sandstone and is 8 centimeters thick. The inside of the stone barrier is divided by two rows of partition stones running east to west, and two sections of corpse beds are set on the north–south and west sides, with round stones the size of a fist laid on the bottom. The central passage between the corpse beds is slightly lower than the corpse beds. Around this stone barrier, some of the piled stones from the walls of the stone chamber remain. The burial chamber was constructed by digging a pit measuring 3.5 meters east to west and 3.2 meters north to south. The entrance to the burial chamber is thought to face west, and there is a V-shaped recess on the west side of the stone barrier. There is also a shallow V-shaped recess in the center of the two rows of partition stones.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The four sides on the stone barrier are decorated with carvings and colors, with four circles on the north side, three circles, two ridges, and two shields on the east side of the back wall, three circles on the south side, and two circles on the west side, all of which are contained within two horizontal lines. The circles are 17-18 centimeters in diameter, with a central hole carved out, two string-like expressions on the top and bottom, and the shield is decorated with patterns, making it important as a stone barrier-type [[decorated kofun]]. There is also a red paint that appears to have been applied to the entire tomb, but its condition is poor. Excavated [[grave goods]] include knives, small beads, and bells, as well as fragments of human bones. The construction and decorative patterns suggest that the tumulus was built around the late 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/> In order to prioritize preservation, not everything was excavated, and damaged stone was treated for preservation before being backfilled.Currently, only the outside of the stone chamber can be viewed under the roof. A life-size replica of the interior of the stone chamber, which cannot be seen on-site, is on display at the Kumamoto Prefectural Decorated Kofuns Museum.
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name =Odara Kofun | native_name =小田良古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Odara Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|38|57|N|130|29|37|E|display=inline, title|ISO =JP-43}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Uki, Kumamoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|38|57|N|130|29|37|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Odara Kofun'''|小田良古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the [[Misumi, Kumamoto|Misumi]] neighborhood of the city of [[Uki, Kumamoto|Uki]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139694|title= 小田良古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Odara Kofun is located at the tip of a low hill close to the coast on the northern coast of the [[Uto Peninsula]], which juts out west from the center of the prefecture. This tumulu is thought to have been an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tumulus with a diameter of about 20 meters, but most of the mound has been lost, and materials for the stone [[burial chamber]] have been piled up. [[Archaeological excavation]]s conducted in 1978 confirmed that the area above the tomb is now farmland 7.2 meters above sea level, and the earth had been removed to the point where the upper edge of the stone barrier around the stone chamber was visible on the field. Four sides of the barrier remain, measuring 1.9 meters east–west and 1.85 meters north–south, with the height to the gravel bed inside being 0.65 meters. The barrier is made of shaped sandstone and is 8 centimeters thick. The inside of the stone barrier is divided by two rows of partition stones running east to west, and two sections of corpse beds are set on the north–south and west sides, with round stones the size of a fist laid on the bottom. The central passage between the corpse beds is slightly lower than the corpse beds. Around this stone barrier, some of the piled stones from the walls of the stone chamber remain. The burial chamber was constructed by digging a pit measuring 3.5 meters east to west and 3.2 meters north to south. The entrance to the burial chamber is thought to face west, and there is a V-shaped recess on the west side of the stone barrier. There is also a shallow V-shaped recess in the center of the two rows of partition stones.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The four sides on the stone barrier are decorated with carvings and colors, with four circles on the north side, three circles, two ridges, and two shields on the east side of the back wall, three circles on the south side, and two circles on the west side, all of which are contained within two horizontal lines. The circles are 17-18 centimeters in diameter, with a central hole carved out, two string-like expressions on the top and bottom, and the shield is decorated with patterns, making it important as a stone barrier-type [[decorated kofun]]. There is also a red paint that appears to have been applied to the entire tomb, but its condition is poor. Excavated [[grave goods]] include knives, small beads, and bells, as well as fragments of human bones. The construction and decorative patterns suggest that the tumulus was built around the late 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/> In order to prioritize preservation, not everything was excavated, and damaged stone was treated for preservation before being backfilled.Currently, only the outside of the stone chamber can be viewed under the roof. A life-size replica of the interior of the stone chamber, which cannot be seen on-site, is on display at the Kumamoto Prefectural Decorated Kofuns Museum.
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Odara Kofun | native_name =小田良古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Odara Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|38|57|N|130|29|37|E|display=inline, title|ISO =JP-43}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Uki, Kumamoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|38|57|N|130|29|37|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Odara Kofun'''|小田良古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the [[Misumi, Kumamoto|Misumi]] neighborhood of the city of [[Uki, Kumamoto|Uki]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139694|title= 小田良古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Odara Kofun is located at the tip of a low hill close to the coast on the northern coast of the [[Uto Peninsula]], which juts out west from the center of the prefecture. This tumulu is thought to have been an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tumulus with a diameter of about 20 meters, but most of the mound has been lost, and materials for the stone [[burial chamber]] have been piled up. [[Archaeological excavation]]s conducted in 1978 confirmed that the area above the tomb is now farmland 7.2 meters above sea level, and the earth had been removed to the point where the upper edge of the stone barrier around the stone chamber was visible on the field. Four sides of the barrier remain, measuring 1.9 meters east–west and 1.85 meters north–south, with the height to the gravel bed inside being 0.65 meters. The barrier is made of shaped sandstone and is 8 centimeters thick. The inside of the stone barrier is divided by two rows of partition stones running east to west, and two sections of corpse beds are set on the north–south and west sides, with round stones the size of a fist laid on the bottom. The central passage between the corpse beds is slightly lower than the corpse beds. Around this stone barrier, some of the piled stones from the walls of the stone chamber remain. The burial chamber was constructed by digging a pit measuring 3.5 meters east to west and 3.2 meters north to south. The entrance to the burial chamber is thought to face west, and there is a V-shaped recess on the west side of the stone barrier. There is also a shallow V-shaped recess in the center of the two rows of partition stones.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The four sides on the stone barrier are decorated with carvings and colors, with four circles on the north side, three circles, two ridges, and two shields on the east side of the back wall, three circles on the south side, and two circles on the west side, all of which are contained within two horizontal lines. The circles are 17-18 centimeters in diameter, with a central hole carved out, two string-like expressions on the top and bottom, and the shield is decorated with patterns, making it important as a stone barrier-type [[decorated kofun]]. There is also a red paint that appears to have been applied to the entire tomb, but its condition is poor. Excavated [[grave goods]] include knives, small beads, and bells, as well as fragments of human bones. The construction and decorative patterns suggest that the tumulus was built around the late 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/> In order to prioritize preservation, not everything was excavated, and damaged stone was treated for preservation before being backfilled.Currently, only the outside of the stone chamber can be viewed under the roof. A life-size replica of the interior of the stone chamber, which cannot be seen on-site, is on display at the Kumamoto Prefectural Decorated Kofuns Museum.
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.uki.kumamoto.jp/kankobunka/bunka/bunkazai/shitei/2268897 Uki City home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://kumamoto.guide/spots/detail/12227 Kumamoto Prefecture Tourist Guide]{{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Odara Kofun]][[ja:小田良古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q109597653]] Wikidata name: Odara Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Odara Kofun | native_name =小田良古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Odara Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|32.649166666666666|N|130.4936111111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Uki, Kumamoto|Uki, Kumamoto]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|38|57|N|130|29|37|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Odara Kofun'''|小田良古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the [[:en:Misumi, Kumamoto|Misumi]] neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Uki, Kumamoto|Uki]], [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139694|title= 小田良古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Odara Kofun is located at the tip of a low hill close to the coast on the northern coast of the [[:en:Uto Peninsula|Uto Peninsula]], which juts out west from the center of the prefecture. This tumulu is thought to have been an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tumulus with a diameter of about 20 meters, but most of the mound has been lost, and materials for the stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] have been piled up. [[Archaeological excavation]]s conducted in 1978 confirmed that the area above the tomb is now farmland 7.2 meters above sea level, and the earth had been removed to the point where the upper edge of the stone barrier around the stone chamber was visible on the field. Four sides of the barrier remain, measuring 1.9 meters east–west and 1.85 meters north–south, with the height to the gravel bed inside being 0.65 meters. The barrier is made of shaped sandstone and is 8 centimeters thick. The inside of the stone barrier is divided by two rows of partition stones running east to west, and two sections of corpse beds are set on the north–south and west sides, with round stones the size of a fist laid on the bottom. The central passage between the corpse beds is slightly lower than the corpse beds. Around this stone barrier, some of the piled stones from the walls of the stone chamber remain. The burial chamber was constructed by digging a pit measuring 3.5 meters east to west and 3.2 meters north to south. The entrance to the burial chamber is thought to face west, and there is a V-shaped recess on the west side of the stone barrier. There is also a shallow V-shaped recess in the center of the two rows of partition stones.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The four sides on the stone barrier are decorated with carvings and colors, with four circles on the north side, three circles, two ridges, and two shields on the east side of the back wall, three circles on the south side, and two circles on the west side, all of which are contained within two horizontal lines. The circles are 17-18 centimeters in diameter, with a central hole carved out, two string-like expressions on the top and bottom, and the shield is decorated with patterns, making it important as a stone barrier-type [[decorated kofun]]. There is also a red paint that appears to have been applied to the entire tomb, but its condition is poor. Excavated [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] include knives, small beads, and bells, as well as fragments of human bones. The construction and decorative patterns suggest that the tumulus was built around the late 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/> In order to prioritize preservation, not everything was excavated, and damaged stone was treated for preservation before being backfilled.Currently, only the outside of the stone chamber can be viewed under the roof. A life-size replica of the interior of the stone chamber, which cannot be seen on-site, is on display at the Kumamoto Prefectural Decorated Kofuns Museum.
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

{{nihongo|'''Odara Kofun'''|小田良古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the [[:en:Misumi, Kumamoto|Misumi]] neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Uki, Kumamoto|Uki]], [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139694|title= 小田良古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Odara Kofun is located at the tip of a low hill close to the coast on the northern coast of the [[:en:Uto Peninsula|Uto Peninsula]], which juts out west from the center of the prefecture. This tumulu is thought to have been an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tumulus with a diameter of about 20 meters, but most of the mound has been lost, and materials for the stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] have been piled up. [[Archaeological excavation]]s conducted in 1978 confirmed that the area above the tomb is now farmland 7.2 meters above sea level, and the earth had been removed to the point where the upper edge of the stone barrier around the stone chamber was visible on the field. Four sides of the barrier remain, measuring 1.9 meters east–west and 1.85 meters north–south, with the height to the gravel bed inside being 0.65 meters. The barrier is made of shaped sandstone and is 8 centimeters thick. The inside of the stone barrier is divided by two rows of partition stones running east to west, and two sections of corpse beds are set on the north–south and west sides, with round stones the size of a fist laid on the bottom. The central passage between the corpse beds is slightly lower than the corpse beds. Around this stone barrier, some of the piled stones from the walls of the stone chamber remain. The burial chamber was constructed by digging a pit measuring 3.5 meters east to west and 3.2 meters north to south. The entrance to the burial chamber is thought to face west, and there is a V-shaped recess on the west side of the stone barrier. There is also a shallow V-shaped recess in the center of the two rows of partition stones.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The four sides on the stone barrier are decorated with carvings and colors, with four circles on the north side, three circles, two ridges, and two shields on the east side of the back wall, three circles on the south side, and two circles on the west side, all of which are contained within two horizontal lines. The circles are 17-18 centimeters in diameter, with a central hole carved out, two string-like expressions on the top and bottom, and the shield is decorated with patterns, making it important as a stone barrier-type [[decorated kofun]]. There is also a red paint that appears to have been applied to the entire tomb, but its condition is poor. Excavated [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] include knives, small beads, and bells, as well as fragments of human bones. The construction and decorative patterns suggest that the tumulus was built around the late 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/> In order to prioritize preservation, not everything was excavated, and damaged stone was treated for preservation before being backfilled.Currently, only the outside of the stone chamber can be viewed under the roof. A life-size replica of the interior of the stone chamber, which cannot be seen on-site, is on display at the Kumamoto Prefectural Decorated Kofuns Museum.
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Odara Kofun]][[ja:小田良古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q109597653]] Wikidata name: Odara Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Odara Kofun | native_name =小田良古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Odara Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|32.649166666666666|N|130.4936111111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Uki, Kumamoto|Uki, Kumamoto]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|38|57|N|130|29|37|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Odara Kofun'''|小田良古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the [[:en:Misumi, Kumamoto|Misumi]] neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Uki, Kumamoto|Uki]], [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139694|title= 小田良古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Odara Kofun is located at the tip of a low hill close to the coast on the northern coast of the [[:en:Uto Peninsula|Uto Peninsula]], which juts out west from the center of the prefecture. This tumulu is thought to have been an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tumulus with a diameter of about 20 meters, but most of the mound has been lost, and materials for the stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] have been piled up. [[Archaeological excavation]]s conducted in 1978 confirmed that the area above the tomb is now farmland 7.2 meters above sea level, and the earth had been removed to the point where the upper edge of the stone barrier around the stone chamber was visible on the field. Four sides of the barrier remain, measuring 1.9 meters east–west and 1.85 meters north–south, with the height to the gravel bed inside being 0.65 meters. The barrier is made of shaped sandstone and is 8 centimeters thick. The inside of the stone barrier is divided by two rows of partition stones running east to west, and two sections of corpse beds are set on the north–south and west sides, with round stones the size of a fist laid on the bottom. The central passage between the corpse beds is slightly lower than the corpse beds. Around this stone barrier, some of the piled stones from the walls of the stone chamber remain. The burial chamber was constructed by digging a pit measuring 3.5 meters east to west and 3.2 meters north to south. The entrance to the burial chamber is thought to face west, and there is a V-shaped recess on the west side of the stone barrier. There is also a shallow V-shaped recess in the center of the two rows of partition stones.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The four sides on the stone barrier are decorated with carvings and colors, with four circles on the north side, three circles, two ridges, and two shields on the east side of the back wall, three circles on the south side, and two circles on the west side, all of which are contained within two horizontal lines. The circles are 17-18 centimeters in diameter, with a central hole carved out, two string-like expressions on the top and bottom, and the shield is decorated with patterns, making it important as a stone barrier-type [[decorated kofun]]. There is also a red paint that appears to have been applied to the entire tomb, but its condition is poor. Excavated [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] include knives, small beads, and bells, as well as fragments of human bones. The construction and decorative patterns suggest that the tumulus was built around the late 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/> In order to prioritize preservation, not everything was excavated, and damaged stone was treated for preservation before being backfilled.Currently, only the outside of the stone chamber can be viewed under the roof. A life-size replica of the interior of the stone chamber, which cannot be seen on-site, is on display at the Kumamoto Prefectural Decorated Kofuns Museum.
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.uki.kumamoto.jp/kankobunka/bunka/bunkazai/shitei/2268897 Uki City home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://kumamoto.guide/spots/detail/12227 Kumamoto Prefecture Tourist Guide]{{in lang|ja}}

{{nihongo|'''Odara Kofun'''|小田良古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the [[:en:Misumi, Kumamoto|Misumi]] neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Uki, Kumamoto|Uki]], [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139694|title= 小田良古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Odara Kofun is located at the tip of a low hill close to the coast on the northern coast of the [[:en:Uto Peninsula|Uto Peninsula]], which juts out west from the center of the prefecture. This tumulu is thought to have been an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style round tumulus with a diameter of about 20 meters, but most of the mound has been lost, and materials for the stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] have been piled up. [[Archaeological excavation]]s conducted in 1978 confirmed that the area above the tomb is now farmland 7.2 meters above sea level, and the earth had been removed to the point where the upper edge of the stone barrier around the stone chamber was visible on the field. Four sides of the barrier remain, measuring 1.9 meters east–west and 1.85 meters north–south, with the height to the gravel bed inside being 0.65 meters. The barrier is made of shaped sandstone and is 8 centimeters thick. The inside of the stone barrier is divided by two rows of partition stones running east to west, and two sections of corpse beds are set on the north–south and west sides, with round stones the size of a fist laid on the bottom. The central passage between the corpse beds is slightly lower than the corpse beds. Around this stone barrier, some of the piled stones from the walls of the stone chamber remain. The burial chamber was constructed by digging a pit measuring 3.5 meters east to west and 3.2 meters north to south. The entrance to the burial chamber is thought to face west, and there is a V-shaped recess on the west side of the stone barrier. There is also a shallow V-shaped recess in the center of the two rows of partition stones.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The four sides on the stone barrier are decorated with carvings and colors, with four circles on the north side, three circles, two ridges, and two shields on the east side of the back wall, three circles on the south side, and two circles on the west side, all of which are contained within two horizontal lines. The circles are 17-18 centimeters in diameter, with a central hole carved out, two string-like expressions on the top and bottom, and the shield is decorated with patterns, making it important as a stone barrier-type [[decorated kofun]]. There is also a red paint that appears to have been applied to the entire tomb, but its condition is poor. Excavated [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] include knives, small beads, and bells, as well as fragments of human bones. The construction and decorative patterns suggest that the tumulus was built around the late 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/> In order to prioritize preservation, not everything was excavated, and damaged stone was treated for preservation before being backfilled.Currently, only the outside of the stone chamber can be viewed under the roof. A life-size replica of the interior of the stone chamber, which cannot be seen on-site, is on display at the Kumamoto Prefectural Decorated Kofuns Museum.
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]
